sections.name: comparison
sections.title: Comparison Section
sections.prompt:
Compare laser cleaning with one traditional cleaning method for {material}. Write naturally, as if discussing this with a colleague.

WRITING APPROACH:
- Focus on practical, real-world differences
- Include specific examples from industry experience
- Use conversational tone with varied sentence structures
- Mention actual challenges technicians face
- Avoid perfectly balanced pros/cons lists
- Include some informal language or industry terminology
- Share insights about when each method works best
- Add context about costs, time, or practical considerations

Write from experience, not from a manual.

sections.name: chart
sections.title: Chart Section
sections.prompt:
Write a chart for {material} comparing at least 4 common cleaning methods, including laser cleaning. The chart should compare efficiency (e.g., m²/hour or another relevant metric). If specific data is unavailable, use plausible, research-based estimates and clearly label each method. Do NOT return placeholders, requests for more information, or incomplete outputs—always generate a complete, context-rich chart suitable for publication.

Your output must be a ready-to-use <script> code block for a bar chart. Use the template structure provided below, but replace the sample data (labels, data values, title) with actual values appropriate for {material}. Maintain the same styling and chart configuration.

Focus on creating realistic, research-based efficiency comparisons that show laser cleaning's advantages while presenting credible data for traditional methods.

sections.name: contaminants
sections.title: Contaminants Section
sections.prompt:
Discuss the typical contaminants found on {material} that require cleaning. Write conversationally, drawing from practical experience.

WRITING APPROACH:
- Focus on real contaminants you'd actually encounter
- Explain why these contaminants are problematic in specific situations
- Use varied sentence structures and lengths
- Include industry-specific terminology naturally
- Mention practical challenges or observations
- Avoid generic lists - provide context about each contaminant
- Write as if sharing knowledge with a peer
- Include some informal language where appropriate

Share insights about which contaminants are most challenging and why.

sections.name: introduction
sections.title: Introduction Section
sections.prompt:
Write a natural, conversational introduction about {material} and laser cleaning. 

WRITING STYLE GUIDELINES:
- Use varied sentence lengths and structures
- Include some informal language or contractions where appropriate
- Add specific, real-world examples or scenarios
- Avoid overly technical jargon without context
- Write as if explaining to a colleague, not a textbook
- Include occasional personal observations or industry insights
- Use natural transitions, not formulaic ones

Focus on practical applications and real industry challenges rather than generic benefits.

sections.name: material_research
sections.title: Material Research Section
sections.prompt:
Given the material: {material}
Provide the following information as a list of key: value pairs, one per line. Use the exact field names below. If a value is unknown, write "N/A". Do NOT write a paragraph or any explanation. Only output the list as shown.

name:
atomicNumber:
chemicalSymbol:
materialType:
metalClass:
crystalStructure:
primaryApplication:
industries: (comma-separated)
keywords: (comma-separated)
applications: (comma-separated)
material_description:

sections.name: substrates
sections.title: Substrates Section
sections.prompt:
Explain the key substrates or base materials associated with {material} in industrial contexts. Write naturally and conversationally.

WRITING STYLE:
- Use varied sentence structures and natural flow
- Include practical context about why these substrates matter
- Share industry insights or common applications
- Avoid rigid numbered lists - integrate information naturally
- Use conversational tone with some informal language
- Provide specific examples where helpful
- Explain relationships between substrates and {material}
- Write as if discussing with a knowledgeable colleague

Focus on practical relevance rather than encyclopedic completeness.

sections.name: table
sections.title: Table Section
sections.prompt:
Create a table with properties of {material} pertinent to laser cleaning. Example:

Cleaning Performance Metrics for Tin

Metric Value Consideration
Removal Rate 7-10 m²/hour Varies with coating thickness
Pulse Energy 0.5-2.0 mJ Lower energy preserves tin substrate
Wavelength 1064 nm Optimal for most tin contaminants
Cleaning Depth 5-50 μm Adjustable based on contamination level
Heat-Affected Zone Minimal (<5 μm) Preserves tin's low melting point properties
